title: centaurea armena
intro: Centaurea armena, also known as Armenian cornflower, is a perennial plant species belonging to the daisy family, native to the mountainous regions of Armenia and Turkey. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of gastrointestinal issues, fever, and respiratory problems due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Centaurea armena include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. Centaurea armena is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
